records.ety: [OE. scoler, AS. scōlere, fr. L. scholaris belonging to a school, fr. schola a school. See School.]
usage: Scholar, Pupil. Scholar refers to the instruction, and pupil to the care and government, of a teacher. A scholar is one who is under instruction; a pupil is one who is under the immediate and personal care of an instructor; hence we speak of a bright scholar, and an obedient pupil.

defs: One who attends a school; one who learns of a teacher; one under the tuition of a preceptor; a pupil; a disciple; a learner; a student.
quotes: I am no breeching scholar in the schools.

defs: One engaged in the pursuits of learning; a learned person; one versed in any branch, or in many branches, of knowledge; a person of high literary or scientific attainments; a savant.
In English universities, an undergraduate who belongs to the foundation of a college, and receives support in part from its revenues.
